query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- Lesson 360022
(360022, 'Who is considered the father of modern hermeneutics?', 'multiple_choice', '["Martin Luther","Friedrich Schleiermacher","John Calvin","Augustine"]', 'Friedrich Schleiermacher', 1),
(360022, 'The Alexandrian school favored which method of interpretation?', 'multiple_choice', '["Literal","Allegorical","Historical","Grammatical"]', 'Allegorical', 2),
(360022, 'Which church father advocated for the fourfold sense of Scripture?', 'multiple_choice', '["Origen","Augustine","Jerome","Chrysostom"]', 'Origen', 3),
(360022, 'The Antiochene school emphasized what approach?', 'multiple_choice', '["Allegorical interpretation","Literal-historical interpretation","Mystical interpretation","Devotional reading"]', 'Literal-historical interpretation', 4),
(360022, 'Martin Luther contributed to hermeneutics by emphasizing what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Papal authority","The perspicuity of Scripture","Tradition over Scripture","Allegorical methods"]', 'The perspicuity of Scripture', 5),
(360022, 'What was the main hermeneutical contribution of the Reformation?', 'multiple_choice', '["Return to allegorical methods","Scripture as its own interpreter","Emphasis on church councils","New translation methods"]', 'Scripture as its own interpreter', 6),
(360022, 'The medieval period was characterized by which interpretive approach?', 'multiple_choice', '["Grammatical-historical","Quadriga (fourfold sense)","Scientific method","Devotional only"]', 'Quadriga (fourfold sense)', 7),
(360022, 'Who emphasized the importance of the original languages for interpretation?', 'multiple_choice', '["Erasmus","Thomas Aquinas","Bernard of Clairvaux","Peter Lombard"]', 'Erasmus', 8),
(360022, 'The Enlightenment brought what challenge to biblical interpretation?', 'multiple_choice', '["Historical criticism","Increased devotion","Return to allegory","Papal authority"]', 'Historical criticism', 9),
(360022, 'Calvin hermeneutical approach emphasized what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Allegorical meanings","Authorial intent and plain meaning","Mystical experiences","Church tradition"]', 'Authorial intent and plain meaning', 10),
(360022, 'Describe how the Reformation changed biblical interpretation and its lasting impact.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The Reformation shifted from church authority to Scripture interprets Scripture, emphasizing plain meaning and original languages.', 11),

-- Lesson 360023
(360023, 'What is the doctrine of biblical inspiration?', 'multiple_choice', '["The Bible contains inspiring stories","God breathed out the Scriptures","Human authors wrote without divine guidance","The Bible is a human product"]', 'God breathed out the Scriptures', 1),
(360023, 'The term theopneustos in 2 Timothy 3:16 means what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Spirit-filled","God-breathed","Divinely inspired","Holy writing"]', 'God-breathed', 2),
(360023, 'Biblical inerrancy refers to what?', 'multiple_choice', '["The Bible is without error in all it affirms","The Bible has no copying errors","The Bible is easy to understand","The Bible is always literal"]', 'The Bible is without error in all it affirms', 3),
(360023, 'What is the relationship between divine inspiration and human authorship?', 'multiple_choice', '["God dictated every word","Human authors wrote without God","Both divine and human elements are present","Only the ideas are inspired"]', 'Both divine and human elements are present', 4),
(360023, 'The canon of Scripture refers to what?', 'multiple_choice', '["The original manuscripts","The recognized authoritative books","The translation method","The interpretation rules"]', 'The recognized authoritative books', 5),
(360023, 'What does biblical sufficiency mean?', 'multiple_choice', '["The Bible is enough for salvation and godly living","The Bible contains all knowledge","The Bible needs no interpretation","The Bible is complete"]', 'The Bible is enough for salvation and godly living', 6),
(360023, 'The authority of Scripture is based on what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Church councils","Its divine origin","Historical accuracy","Popular acceptance"]', 'Its divine origin', 7),
(360023, 'What is verbal plenary inspiration?', 'multiple_choice', '["Only the words are inspired","Only the ideas are inspired","All words and all parts are fully inspired","Inspiration varies by book"]', 'All words and all parts are fully inspired', 8),
(360023, 'How does the doctrine of preservation relate to Scripture?', 'multiple_choice', '["God has preserved His Word through history","Original manuscripts still exist","Translations are perfect","Copying errors do not exist"]', 'God has preserved His Word through history', 9),
(360023, 'The Chicago Statement on Biblical Inerrancy addresses what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Translation methods","The nature and authority of Scripture","Preaching techniques","Church governance"]', 'The nature and authority of Scripture', 10),
(360023, 'Explain how the dual authorship of Scripture affects our approach to interpretation.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'We must consider both the divine message and human context, including historical situation and literary style.', 11),

-- Lesson 360024
(360024, 'What is the grammatical-historical method?', 'multiple_choice', '["Studying grammar only","Interpreting based on grammar and historical context","A modern invention","Allegorical interpretation"]', 'Interpreting based on grammar and historical context', 1),
(360024, 'Why is understanding the original language important?', 'multiple_choice', '["Translations are unreliable","Words have nuances lost in translation","Only scholars can interpret","Hebrew is sacred"]', 'Words have nuances lost in translation', 2),
(360024, 'What is a hapax legomenon?', 'multiple_choice', '["A common word","A word appearing only once","A Hebrew verb","A Greek noun"]', 'A word appearing only once', 3),
(360024, 'Context in interpretation includes what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Only the immediate verse","Literary, historical, and cultural context","Personal experience","Modern application"]', 'Literary, historical, and cultural context', 4),
(360024, 'What is a word study?', 'multiple_choice', '["Memorizing definitions","Examining a word usage and meaning across Scripture","Learning Greek","Reading dictionaries"]', 'Examining a word usage and meaning across Scripture', 5),
(360024, 'The historical context helps us understand what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Modern applications","The original situation and meaning","Translation choices","Church traditions"]', 'The original situation and meaning', 6),
(360024, 'What is syntactical analysis?', 'multiple_choice', '["Studying word meanings","Examining sentence structure and relationships","Learning vocabulary","Reading commentaries"]', 'Examining sentence structure and relationships', 7),
(360024, 'Why must we avoid the root fallacy?', 'multiple_choice', '["Roots are unimportant","Word meaning comes from usage, not just etymology","Etymology is always wrong","Greek has no roots"]', 'Word meaning comes from usage, not just etymology', 8),
(360024, 'What role does archaeology play in interpretation?', 'multiple_choice', '["It replaces Scripture","It illuminates historical and cultural context","It proves the Bible","It is unnecessary"]', 'It illuminates historical and cultural context', 9),
(360024, 'The principle of Scripture interprets Scripture means what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Unclear passages are explained by clearer ones","All passages mean the same thing","We do not need study","Only one interpretation exists"]', 'Unclear passages are explained by clearer ones', 10),
(360024, 'Describe the steps involved in conducting a proper word study.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Identify the original word, examine its usage across Scripture, consider context, and determine specific meaning.', 11),

-- Lesson 360025
(360025, 'What are the main literary genres in the Bible?', 'multiple_choice', '["Only narrative","Narrative, poetry, prophecy, wisdom, apocalyptic, epistle","Only letters","Only history"]', 'Narrative, poetry, prophecy, wisdom, apocalyptic, epistle', 1),
(360025, 'How should we interpret Hebrew poetry?', 'multiple_choice', '["Always literally","Recognizing parallelism and figurative language","As historical narrative","As prophecy"]', 'Recognizing parallelism and figurative language', 2),
(360025, 'What is apocalyptic literature characterized by?', 'multiple_choice', '["Plain language","Symbolic imagery and visions","Historical narrative","Legal codes"]', 'Symbolic imagery and visions', 3),
(360025, 'Parables should be interpreted how?', 'multiple_choice', '["Every detail has meaning","Finding the main point","As literal history","As allegory"]', 'Finding the main point', 4),
(360025, 'What distinguishes wisdom literature?', 'multiple_choice', '["Prophecies about the future","Practical guidance for godly living","Historical accounts","Legal requirements"]', 'Practical guidance for godly living', 5),
(360025, 'Epistles are best understood by considering what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Only the theology","The occasion, recipients, and purpose","Universal application only","Modern context"]', 'The occasion, recipients, and purpose', 6),
(360025, 'Narrative passages teach theology through what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Direct commands","Story and example","Propositional statements","Legal codes"]', 'Story and example', 7),
(360025, 'What is the danger of allegorizing narrative?', 'multiple_choice', '["It is always correct","It can impose meanings not intended by the author","It is the only method","Narratives have no meaning"]', 'It can impose meanings not intended by the author', 8),
(360025, 'Prophetic literature often contains what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Only future predictions","Both forth-telling and foretelling","Only historical accounts","Only poetry"]', 'Both forth-telling and foretelling', 9),
(360025, 'How do we move from interpretation to application?', 'multiple_choice', '["Skip interpretation","Find the principle and apply it appropriately","Apply everything literally","Ignore cultural context"]', 'Find the principle and apply it appropriately', 10),
(360025, 'Explain why recognizing literary genre is essential for proper biblical interpretation.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Different genres have different interpretive rules. Misidentifying genre leads to misinterpretation.', 11);
